meta.title: Convert MP4 to WebM — Free, In Your Browser, No Upload | {{SITE_NAME}}
meta.description: Convert .mp4 video to .webm right in your browser. Smaller files for the web, nothing uploaded — your video never leaves your device. Free, no watermark.
og.title: Convert MP4 to WebM — Free, No Upload
og.description: Change .mp4 to .webm locally in your browser. Nothing leaves your device.
hero.title: Convert MP4 to WebM — right in your browser
hero.lead: Drop an .mp4 and get a .webm — the open format built for the web, often smaller at the same quality. It's converted on your own device and never uploaded.
prose.p1: WebM is the open, royalty-free format designed for the web. It tends to be smaller than MP4 at similar quality and is ideal for background videos, HTML5 <video> and places where you want a lightweight, license-free file.
prose.p2: Most converters upload your clip to a server first. {{SITE_NAME}} doesn't — the conversion runs locally in your browser. WebM uses the built-in software engine, so it's slower than the MP4 fast path, but nothing is ever stored or logged.
faq.a1: No. The conversion happens entirely in your browser, on your own device. There's no server that could receive the file — watch your browser's Network tab while it runs and you'll see zero bytes leave.
faq.a2: WebM uses a software encoder that browsers can't hardware-accelerate for output, so it's slower than converting to MP4. Keep clips short for the quickest results.
faq.a3: WebM plays in all modern browsers and on Android, but not on every older device or in some editors. If you need maximum compatibility instead, convert to MP4.
